message:
fix(update/lock): use O_CREAT|O_EXCL to eliminate concurrent-acquirer race

UpdateLock::acquire() did an exists-check + File::create (which
truncates) two-step. Two acquirers racing past a stale-lock sweep
could both call File::create, both truncate the file, and both write
their own PIDs — both believing they held the lock while one
silently overwrote the other.

Switch to OpenOptions::create_new (O_CREAT|O_EXCL) so the lock-file
creation itself is the mutual-exclusion primitive. When the exclusive
create fails with AlreadyExists:

- If the file contains a live PID, return UpdateError::LockHeld.
- If the file points at a dead PID (stale), attempt a one-shot
  compare-and-remove sweep and retry the exclusive create exactly
  once. If the retry still loses the create race, treat the lock as
  held — someone else won.

Also sync_all the lock file after writing our PID (so crash-inspect
after poweroff still sees the right PID) and clean up the half-
written lock on write/sync failure so the next acquirer doesn't see
a malformed PID and choke on the sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
